answer.text: Their time value decreases with the passage of time and eventually becomes zero at expiry
The underlying currency loses value over time
Trading costs erode profits over time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Options lose time value every day (theta/time decay). At expiry, time value = 0 and the option is worth only its intrinsic value. This inherent time decay makes options 'wasting assets'. **The Trap:** Not ALL options expire worthless — ITM options have intrinsic value at expiry. But all options lose time value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Time decay concept is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why option sellers have a structural advantage — time works in their favor. Option buyers need the underlying to move enough to overcome time decay.
